source_jobs.title: Staff Data Platform Engineer
Staff Software Engineer, Backend
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE:

As a Staff Backend Engineer at FloSports, you’ll be a key technical leader shaping how our backend systems evolve. You’ll take on complex, high-impact initiatives that strengthen our platforms — particularly around distributed systems, identity, and service reliability.



This role goes beyond writing great code. You’ll help define the technical direction of major platform components, mentor senior engineers, and work closely with product and leadership to design solutions that scale across our ecosystem. You’ll bring deep experience in authentication and authorization frameworks (OAuth, OIDC, RBAC/ABAC) and in the architecture and modernization of distributed identity and access systems, paired with strong technical expertise in TypeScript, Node.js, and modern frameworks like NestJS and Fastify, as well as a solid foundation in cloud-based architecture on AWS.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Drive modernization efforts across our platform — improving how identity, access, and authorization are handled across systems.

 - Lead architecture and development of backend services that are scalable, reliable, and easy to maintain.

 - Design APIs and event-driven workflows that enable clear communication between services and products.

 - Provide technical mentorship, guidance, and code reviews that raise the bar for the entire engineering team.

 - Leverage AI-driven tools and automation to improve quality, consistency, and development velocity.

 - Build and maintain backend services using TypeScript, NestJS, and Node.js, ensuring strong testing practices with Jest, Vitest, and Supertest.

 - Apply a strong understanding of AWS services (Aurora, EventBridge, Lambda, DynamoDB, S3 etc..) to design reliable and performant distributed systems.

 - Partner with engineering and product teams to deliver technical solutions that support key business goals.

 - Champion best practices around security, testing, CI/CD, observability, and service ownership.



KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:

 - Minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree or foreign equivalent in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Software Engineering, Information Systems, Data Science or a related field

 - Extensive experience designing and scaling distributed systems and service-oriented architectures.

 - Deep understanding of authentication and authorization frameworks (OAuth 2.0, OIDC, RBAC/ABAC) and experience modernizing or re-platforming identity and entitlement systems.

 - Strong proficiency in TypeScript and Node.js, including advanced design patterns and maintainable code practices.

 - Experience with both SQL and NoSQL databases (DynamoDB, Aurora, Firestore).

 - Proven success building and maintaining RESTful APIs and event-driven systems.

 - Solid understanding of AWS services and cloud-native architecture.

 - Strong testing discipline and familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and automation workflows.

 - Demonstrated technical leadership and ability to mentor engineers effectively.

 - Experience using AI-assisted development tools to improve engineeri

source_jobs.title: Senior Native Application Engineer
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE

We are seeking a Senior Native Apps Engineer to join our mobile and TV platform squad, building and evolving the streaming experiences that bring fans closer to the sports they love. This role is primarily hands-on, writing native iOS and Android code, with the expectation of contributing across our TV platforms (Apple TV, Fire TV/Android TV, Roku) as needed. The ideal candidate brings deep expertise in at least one mobile platform, a willingness to work across all of them, and a strong command of AI-assisted development workflows that accelerate delivery and elevate team productivity. This is a high-growth position with a clear path toward Staff-level engineering leadership.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop and maintain native iOS and Android applications, delivering high-quality streaming experiences at scale.

 - Contribute to TV platform applications (Apple TV, Fire TV/Android TV, Roku) alongside a team of cross-platform engineers.

 - Partner with BFF engineers to define API contracts and server-driven UI patterns that power the native experience layer.

 - Leverage AI-assisted development tools to accelerate personal and team productivity, and champion adoption of these workflows across the squad.

 - Drive architectural decisions for the native platform, establishing shared patterns, reusable components, and cross-platform consistency.

 - Partner with product, design, and engineering leadership to translate business objectives into well-scoped technical work.

 - Collaborate closely with team members and contractors, contributing to quality standards and knowledge sharing across the squad.

 - Identify and address technical debt that impacts platform stability, performance, or developer velocity.

 - Champion best practices around app security, crash reporting, observability, and release quality.



KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES

 - Strong proficiency in Swift and Kotlin with hands-on experience shipping production mobile applications.

 - Deep understanding of native mobile development patterns, architecture, and platform-specific best practices.

 - Proficiency with AI-assisted development tools (e.g., GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor) and a track record of using them to accelerate delivery.

 - Familiarity with live streaming, video playback, or media-rich application development.

 - Experience working across multiple platforms or a demonstrated willingness to learn new ones.

 - Strong understanding of RESTful APIs and experience collaborating with backend teams on API design and integration.

 - Familiarity with app performance monitoring, crash reporting, and observability tooling.

 - Familiarity with mobile CI/CD pipelines and tooling (e.g., Fastlane, Bitrise, Xcode Cloud).

 - Ownership mindset with a bias for action and a drive to solve hard problems.

 - Excellent communication skills and ability to work effectively in a team with a mix of FTEs and contractors.

 - A growth-oriented mindset with curiosity to explore new technologies and platforms.



BONUS POINTS

 - Experience with

source_jobs.title: Live Stream Associate I Seasonal
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



 

THE ROLE:

FloSports is looking for a Part Time, Live Stream Associate I to join our Operations department. The ideal candidate will be an enthusiastic learner, some video experience, with a passion for providing high-quality live event coverage. This role will remotely support and manage multiple events simultaneously by helping check and adjust streaming settings, managing start and end times, monitoring commentary, graphics, and other treatments. Ideal candidates will be strong communicators and have a keen eye for detail.



 

This position will be based in Waterloo, ON.

CAD - seasonal part time employment (Aug 2026 - April 2027)



RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Identify and correct audio and video quality issues to ensure client satisfaction is achieved

 - Ensure events are live and maintain high stream uptime and quality

 - Communicate key information with other technicians, camera operators, supporting team members, and managers

 - Understand and respond to client concerns and issues with video streams

 - Commonly work weekends & evenings

 - Other tasks as assigned by your managers



 

KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:

 - Ability to multitask

 - Strong focus and attention to detail

 - Tech-savvy, have an eye for quality, and be a sports enthusiast.

 - Process driven and detail oriented

 - Strong organizational skills

 - Excellent communication skills



 

SCHEDULE REQUIREMENTS

 - Must be available to work Friday, Saturday & Sunday

 - Must be available to work from evenings & weekends

 - Operating hours 7am - 10pm (8 hour shifts)



BONUS POINTS:

 - Knowledge of the following technologies:
   
   - Encoders
   
   - Youbora
   
   - H.264/H.265
   
   - HLS/RTMP/SRT
   
   - TVU
   
   - LiveU
   
   - vMix
   
   - Cameras
   
   - Live Streaming
   
   - Audio and Sound Mixing



OUR COMMITMENT TO DIVERSITY:

FloSports exists to elevate the communities, athletes, and sports that have too often been overlooked. That mission starts with our own team. We believe that a diverse, inclusive workplace—one where different perspectives are sought out, heard, and valued—is essential to building a company that can truly serve the full spectrum of sports fans.



We are committed to creating a fair and equitable environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive. To help mitigate bias and expand opportunity, FloSports uses a blind recruiting process that supports more objective, inclusive hiring decisions. We’re intentional in our practices because we believe the best ideas and innovations come from teams that reflect the diversity of the world around us.



 

OUR BENEFITS CAD:

 - Recognized three years in a row as a Top Workplace by the Austin-American Statesman

 - Vacation pay calculated and added to each paycheque at 4%

 - Stocked snack bar, catered lunch every week

 - FloFun Social Events each month

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE:

As Engineering Manager, you will be responsible for leading and growing a high-performing team of talented fullstack and backend software engineers. You’ll be relied upon to help drive the strategic vision of our applications alongside our Product Managers and Designers.



Additionally, you’ll have an impact on elevating the entire Product Engineering Department through close collaboration with others at the leadership level—driving inclusion, diversity, ownership, and autonomy.



We are open to fully remote candidates for this role with occasional travel to our Austin, TX office.



Our tech stack includes: TypeScript, HTML/CSS/JS, PHP, MySQL, Redis, Sphinx, Firebase, Git/GitHub, Docker, EC2, S3, SNS, SQS, DataDog, and PagerDuty



RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Manage the performance, development and engagement of software engineers (4-6 direct reports) including hiring and growing of a high-performance team in shipping full stack web applications, features and services

 - Drives execution by organizing team work, setting goals, and holding the team accountable, including monitoring and escalating team issues

 - Ability to collaborate with Product Owners, designers, and QA on scheduling, planning, and sizing of functional specifications and software designs

 - Actively work against bottlenecks impacting the team so they can make progress towards team and department goals

 - Responsible for staying familiar with industry advances that may improve the team’s ability to execute more efficiently

 - Responsible for the quality and technical output of the team

 - Recruit, interview, and onboard new team members

 - Help to foster a culture of growth, openness, and opportunities for your team, including regular collaboration with other Engineering Leaders to drive accountability, autonomy, inclusion, diversity, and performance across the department.

 - Participate in code reviews, technical/architectural design and discussions, ensuring teams have healthy on-call escalations and incident management



KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:

 - 4+ years’ experience doing software engineering in a hands-on capacity - front-end and back-end focus

 - 2+ years management and / or team leadership experience in a software development role

 - Experience in leading a team of software engineer, helping them improve their skills, grow their careers, hiring and performance management

 - Demonstrated ability to focus on process improvements and key performance metrics that add to the overall effectiveness of the development team

 - Able to collaborate effectively and act as a partner in product decisions

 - Ability to understand systems and software architecture similar to a Sr. level engineer with considerations towards high traffic scalable web and distributed systems with an eye towards improving quality, reliability, performance and scalability

 - Proficiency in Front-End and Backend-End technologies

 - Proficient in consuming and building RESTful APIs with JSON and CRUD

 - High level proficiency with CI/CD processes

source_jobs.title: Staff Site Reliability Engineer
source_jobs.description:
FloSports exists to elevate the communities, athletes, and sports that have too often been overlooked. That mission starts with our own team. We believe that a diverse, inclusive workplace—one where different perspectives are sought out, heard, and valued—is essential to building a company that can truly serve the full spectrum of sports fans.



We are committed to creating a fair and equitable environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive. To help mitigate bias and expand opportunity, FloSports uses a blind recruiting process that supports more objective, inclusive hiring decisions. We’re intentional in our practices because we believe the best ideas and innovations come from teams that reflect the diversity of the world around us.



THE ROLE: 

At FloSports, SRE is the team that acts as a force multiplier for our engineering organization. Our mission is to be the "wind in the sails" for our developers, enabling them to ship features faster, safer, and with more confidence. We are a "code-first" group that believes in automating away toil and solving problems with software. We don't click buttons in a console; we write code, build tools, and manage our infrastructure through GitOps.



As a Staff SRE, you will be a technical leader on a highly skilled and senior team. You will be a key driver of our architecture, reliability, and developer enablement strategy. This role requires a balance of high-impact individual contribution, technical leadership, and close collaboration with other Staff and Senior engineers to set the technical direction for the entire organization.



Our culture is built on principles of shared stability responsibility and pragmatism. We are guided by a philosophy of simplicity (if you've read grugbrain.dev http://grugbrain.dev, you'll fit right in). We believe it's more fun to be competent, and we're looking for another expert to join our team.



RESPONSIBILITIES: 

 - Lead the technical architecture and execution of our landmark migration from a legacy GCP environment to a modern, scalable infrastructure on AWS EKS.

 - Architect, design, and drive our core infrastructure, defining the patterns for Terraform and GitOps that the rest of the organization will follow.

 - Champion and drive our SLO-driven culture, setting the strategy for how we define, measure, and implement SLOs for critical user journeys, guided by the four Golden Signals (Latency, Traffic, Errors, and Saturation).

 - Lead the design and development of critical tooling and automation in Node.js and Go to solve entire classes of problems for our developers.

 - Lead the architectural evolution of our in-house, K6-based load testing platform, ensuring it can scale to meet future product demands.

 - Act as a primary subject matter expert for our Istio service mesh, driving its architecture, adoption, and optimization.

 - Spearhead and own high-priority initiatives, including the development of agentic workflows and intelligent automation for SRE domains like proactive scaling and automated remediation.

Act as a technical leader by participating in our blameless on-call rotation, mentoring other engineers through complex incidents and ensuring all post-mortems lead to systemic, long-term improvements.



KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:  

 - Extensive Experience: 8-10+ years in SRE, DevOps, or Software Engineering, with a proven track record of operating at a Staff level.

 - Proven Technical Leadership: You have a history of mentoring other senior engineers, influencing technical direction across multiple teams, and leading large-scale projects to completion.

 - Expert Coder: You are a polyglot with deep expertise in languages like Node.js or Go and a history of building and maintaining critical automation and services.

 - Kubernetes Architect: You have an expert-level, architectural understanding of Kubernetes (EKS preferred), including networking, custom controllers, and control plane optimization.

 - Infrastructur

source_jobs.title: Sales Strategist
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE:

FloSports is currently looking for an Ad Sales Strategist to work within our sales & partnerships division developing custom, 360 programs for the company’s advertising partners. This position will be responsible for creating integrated campaigns that meet the needs of existing and potential partners and include sponsorships, experiential, branded content and media.



The Sales Strategist will be tasked with developing compelling, custom data-backed partnerships to maximize revenue and drive client retention.



Creating collateral, developing custom programs/solutions, forecasting inventory, building media plans, and sourcing pertinent data to meet the specific KPIs of each partner.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Leverage industry and internal knowledge to bridge FloSports’ creative capabilities and offerings to our prospects marketing objectives

 - Lead the entire RFP process from intake + initial brainstorm to final submission and any subsequent revisions

 - Maintain solid knowledge of industry standards, and proactively seek out new media and sponsorship opportunities across FloSports, leveraging market research and competitive analysis

 - Develop RFP responses, leveraging target market research data, performance data, custom mocks, and effective storytelling to deliver a custom program that meets client needs

 - Leverage 1st and 3rd party data sources to help drive client conversations forward and enhance sales materials

 - Maintain knowledge of FloSports internal content offerings ranging from O&O Events, activations, podcasts/shows and content executions/plans

 - Work directly with the Integrated Partnerships sales team to generate proactive collateral for clients and agencies, including but not limited to: case studies, one-sheets, media kits, 101 decks, custom pitch decks, etc.

 - Leverage design knowledge for development of sales assets and proposal presentations

 - Act as the liaison between the sales org and multiple departments including content, marketing, design, production vetting and brainstorming advertising solutions

 - Utilize sales lead generation platforms to identify partner prospects and leads for sales team

 - Generate media plans, recommending ad placements and content channels based on client’s campaign objectives

 - Assist in the development of media, branded content and sponsorship pricing



KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:

 - 2-4 years of experience within sport industry around sponsorship development, sales strategy development and digital media

 - Demonstrate the ability to develop creative ideas and solutions

 - Knowledgeable in the sports sponsorship industry, with insights into recent spending trends and opportunities

 - Experienced in proposing, planning, evaluating, or pitching marketing campaigns

 - Experienced using 3rd party audience measurement platforms (comScore, Nielson, Quantcast, Google Analytics, etc.)

 - Experience in proposal building and layout (Canva, PPT, Google Slides, etc.)

 - Well-versed in digital media marketing platforms, including soc

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE:

We are seeking a Senior Full Stack Developer to build and evolve scalable web applications, balancing frontend and backend development. This role emphasizes hands-on coding, collaboration, and system design to deliver high-performance, maintainable solutions. The ideal candidate is a strong software engineer who values clean architecture, thoughtful design, and pragmatic problem-solving.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Design, develop, and maintain scalable, high-performance web applications across the full stack.

 - Build reusable UI components and libraries with attention to detail and user experience.

 - Develop backend services and APIs, ensuring seamless integration with frontend applications.

 - Contribute to architectural decisions within your team.

 - Participate in code reviews and support the technical growth of peers.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define requirements, estimate work, and meet project objectives.

 - Identify technical debt and advocate for improvements that balance velocity with code quality.



KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:

 - Strong foundation in software engineering principles, design patterns, and clean code practices.

 - Proficient in TypeScript and modern frontend frameworks (React, Angular, Vue, or similar).

 - Skilled in Node.js and building high-performance APIs.

 - Experience with responsive, mobile-first web application development.

 - Familiarity with monorepo tooling, OOP principles, and scalable architecture.

 - Experience designing solutions for complex systems, such as ad delivery networks or authentication.

 - Ownership mindset with a drive for results and creative problem-solving.

 - Strong communication skills with the ability to align technical work with business objectives.



NICE TO HAVE:

 - Experience with Angular and NestJS.

 - Familiarity with NX monorepos.

FloSports exists to elevate the communities, athletes, and sports that have too often been overlooked. That mission starts with our own team. We believe that a diverse, inclusive workplace—one where different perspectives are sought out, heard, and valued—is essential to building a company that can truly serve the full spectrum of sports fans.



We are committed to creating a fair and equitable environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive. To help mitigate bias and expand opportunity, FloSports uses a blind recruiting process that supports more objective, inclusive hiring decisions. We’re intentional in our practices because we believe the best ideas and innovations come from teams that reflect the diversity of the world around us.




OUR BENEFITS:

 - Recognized three years in a row as a Top Workplace by the Austin-American Statesman

 - Flexibility at work - you can take control of your profession and personal schedule

 - All-hands events hosted annually in beautiful Austin, Texas

 - Annual equity awards for all top performers

 - Competitive and comprehensive medical, dental and vision plans

source_jobs.title: Live Event Producer
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE:

FloSports is looking for a Live Events Producer to join our Production team. A FloSports Producer is on the front lines creating our most valuable product and the ideal candidate will be an enthusiastic, talented, highly-technical live broadcast professional with a passion for providing high-quality live event coverage. This role will lead setup and execution in the field, and excel in handling the curveballs that come from live sports production. The Producer will develop the rundown, oversee pre-planning, prep the crew and commentary team with the storylines and coverage plan, and ensure our equipment inventory is received and returned in working order. You’ll think quickly on your feet and adapt to the live story being told. You are the eyes and ears of our customer. This position is a road-warrior requiring an ability to travel up to 80% and work evenings/weekends/holidays.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Travel regularly around the country to produce broadcasts for various events including wrestling, grappling, track & field, marching band, cheerleading, football, basketball, softball, and baseball among others. International travel is also a possibility.

 - Work closely with internal departments and external partners to coordinate broadcast needs.

 - Work closely with and help manage production team personnel, including event directors, producers, on-air talent, editors, graphics designers and operators, scoring systems personnel.

 - Ready to step into multiple roles depending on the show execution

 - Identify and share best practices. Create and maintain rundowns and tech books.

 - Participate in and organize an efficient technical setup and tear down.

 - Perform thorough inventory checks both before and after each show.

 - Take ownership of production quality and help to drive improvements.

 - Fully invested in every stage of the production process, from pre-production, the live broadcast, post-event review and event recaps.

 - Manage production planning details through a task tracker, schedules, establish timelines and create event documentation, including tech books.

 - Troubleshoot technology issues and production problems logically, calmly and efficiently. Escalate issues as needed.

 - Take ownership of production quality and help to drive improvements

 - Build and maintain relationships with partners, vendors, crew, and other event stakeholders.



KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:

 - Familiarity with on-site, remote, and cloud based production models.

 - Proven experience producing events in a live streaming or broadcast environment.

 - Experience with storytelling, editing, shooting, lighting, and audio.

 - Critical thinking and troubleshooting experience.

 - Ability to lead, execute and deliver in a fast-paced, sometimes stressful environment.

 - Familiarity with software based production tools, such as OBS and vMix, as well as Blackmagic ATEM.

 - Familiarity with Prosumer level cameras (i.e. Panasonic CX350 and CX10, Marshall POV cameras, Panasonic PTZ, etc.) and fiber optic cab

source_jobs.title: Senior Engineer, Live Streaming Video Specialist
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE:

You will play a key role in designing, building, and operating the live streaming platform at FloSports, delivering tens of thousands of live events every year to fans around the world. This is a role for a proven video specialist: we expect demonstrated, hands-on experience operating live broadcast video at scale in production, spanning contribution, transcoding, packaging, and delivery across many concurrent events. You'll own the backend services and APIs behind that pipeline end-to-end using Node.js/NestJS and/or Go, and you'll be accountable for keeping the full live signal chain reliable under real-world broadcast conditions, including redundancy and graceful failover. Experience with AWS Elemental Media Services is a strong plus.



As a senior member of the team, you will also participate in an on-call rotation to help ensure the reliability of our live streaming platform.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Operate, automate, and continuously improve the live broadcast video pipeline (contribution, transcoding, packaging, and delivery) for tens of thousands of events per year, with a focus on performance, scalability, and broadcast-grade reliability.

 - Monitor and safeguard stream health and viewer quality of experience (QoE) at scale, building the tooling and alerting needed to catch issues before they reach audiences.

 - Design and implement data models and queries for SQL databases.

 - Build and maintain backend microservices using Node.js/NestJS and/or Go.

 - Write and execute unit and integration tests using tools like Jest, and Vitest to ensure high code quality.

 - Write clean, maintainable, and well-tested code, following best practices.

 - Participate in code reviews, fostering a culture of continuous learning and improvement.

 - Collaborate closely with product managers, and other stakeholders to define and deliver new features.

 - Diagnose and resolve live broadcast and streaming pipeline issues in real time, including during live events, and drive lasting improvements to redundancy and failover.



KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:

 - Demonstrated hands-on experience operating live broadcast video at scale in production, including multi-event, multi-channel contribution, transcoding, packaging, and delivery.

 - Deep understanding of the live broadcast signal chain, including redundancy, failover, and graceful degradation under real-world live conditions.

 - Hands-on experience with live streaming transport protocols (SRT, RTMP, Zixi, WebRTC) and how they integrate with media pipelines.

 - Proficiency in TypeScript, Node.js and/or Go, including a deep understanding of language features and design patterns.

 - Experience working with SQL databases.

 - Experience building and maintaining RESTful APIs.

 - Familiarity with NestJS or similar backend frameworks.

 - Hands-on experience writing and executing unit tests and integration tests using Jest, and Vitest.

 - Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and integrating tests into deployment workflows.

 - Familiarity with cloud infrastructure

source_jobs.title: Customer Service Representative, French Bilingual
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE:

We are seeking a customer-focused, tech-savvy, and bilingual (Canadian French/English) Customer Support Representative to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will serve as the first point of contact for customers, providing timely and professional support via phone and email. You will troubleshoot technical issues, guide partners on platform usage, and collaborate across departments to ensure a seamless customer experience. This position requires strong communication skills, flexibility with rotating shifts, and a passion for problem-solving in a fast-paced, sports-centric environment.

 

RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Deliver timely, friendly, and professional support to customers via phone and email

 - Troubleshoot and resolve customer and partner issues in real time

 - Maintain and update customer accounts, including subscriptions and billing, following department policies

 - Meet or exceed all assigned individual and team performance metrics
   
    

PROCESSES

 - When requested, collaborate with the Team Lead and/or Customer Support Manager to:

 - Escalate processes that may require updates or changes

 - Document process modifications for future reference and training

 - Recommend workflow improvements to enhance efficiency

 - Suggest strategies to improve response times and overall customer experience

 

EXPECTATIONS

 - Must be available for rotating shifts, including days, evenings, weekends, and holidays

 - Shift times will vary based on operational needs and streaming schedules

 - Ability to multitask and perform effectively in a fast-paced environment

 - Attend weekly customer support team meetings

 - Willingness to take on other duties as assigned

 

LANGUAGE PROFICIENCY

 - Fluent in Canadian French (spoken, written, and reading)

 - Skilled at adjusting tone and vocabulary to match various customer profiles, including both formal and informal communication styles

 

KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES

 - Minimum of two years of customer service experience (preferred)

 - Strong problem-solving skills with a proactive, solution-oriented mindset

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills

 - Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to work effectively in a team

 - Proficient with computers and comfortable using various technical tools

 - Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment

 - Flexibility to work a variety of shifts, including days, evenings, and weekends

 

BONUS POINTS

 - Familiarity with WordPress or LeagueStat

 - Experience using customer support (CS) or other ticketing systems (e.g., Zendesk, Freshdesk)

 - Backgroun

source_jobs.title: Programming Coordinator - Contract
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE:

FloSports, a leader and innovator in digital sports media, is currently seeking a Programming Coordinator to join our Operations team. The primary responsibilities include maintaining our calendar of live events and event critical details across various internal and external systems for all of our rights partnerships. This person will work cross-departmentally to gather necessary information of our live broadcasts and will continually seek out ways to provide a seamless flow of data from prospective event to live stream. This position is based in Austin, TX and reports to the Sr. Manager of Programming.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Communicate with our partners and work cross-departmentally to ensure every event and the critical details (dates, production specifications…etc) of all potential and signed streaming agreements are entered and maintained across the FloSports platform

 - Advocates for and oversees process improvements around live broadcast scheduling workflows

 - Assists in strategic planning and scheduling of all our sports broadcasts

 - Perform data cleansing, data uploads and data exports within SalesForce

 - Develop and create customized reports and dashboards

 - Directly and indirectly through team, sets and ensures the highest levels of professionalism in all aspects



KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & ABILITIES:

 - Must possess strong communication skills and the proven ability to build strong relationships.

 - Proficient in Excel and strong data manipulation skills- must be comfortable working with large amounts of data.

 - Ability to prioritize, get things done accurately and manage multiple projects with ease.

 - Basic knowledge of the sports media industry.

 - Tech savvy and able to thrive in a fast paced, team environment.

 - Bonus points for experience with SalesForce and/or familiarity with reading and comprehending legal contracts is preferred.

 - Bachelor's degree required.



OUR COMMITMENT TO DIVERSITY:

FloSports exists to elevate the communities, athletes, and sports that have too often been overlooked. That mission starts with our own team. We believe that a diverse, inclusive workplace—one where different perspectives are sought out, heard, and valued—is essential to building a company that can truly serve the full spectrum of sports fans.



We are committed to creating a fair and equitable environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive. To help mitigate bias and expand opportunity, FloSports uses a blind recruiting process that supports more objective, inclusive hiring decisions. We’re intentional in our practices because we believe the best ideas and innovations come from teams that reflect the diversity of the world around us.

source_jobs.title: Fleet Operations Dispatch Coordinator - Part-Time
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE:

FloSports is seeking a part time Fleet Operations Dispatch Coordinator to support the daily operation, readiness, compliance, and travel coordination of our transportation fleet based in Bloomington, Indiana, where our trailers are located. This role will serve as the central point of coordination for fleet logistics, driver support, DOT compliance, vehicle and trailer maintenance scheduling, documentation, and incident response and work in Austin, TX.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Coordinate daily fleet operations remotely from our Austin, TX office for FloSports vehicles and trailers based in Bloomington, Indiana.

 - Serve as the first point of coordination for schedule and travel fluidity, including responding to changing weather, hotel changes, route issues, timing adjustments, and other operational shifts.

 - Keep travel and operational tracking documents accurate and up to date, including sheets, rolling costs, and related scheduling records.

 - Maintain awareness of where drivers and trailers are located between shows and help ensure clear communication across operations.

 - Coordinate parking and storage logistics for trailers and fleet assets at storage sites.

 - Support and coordinate driver hiring, onboarding, and recordkeeping in partnership with internal stakeholders.

 - Prepare and distribute regular fleet updates or scorecards to leadership, similar to a weekly dispatcher scorecard model.

 - Coordinate driver travel logistics and scheduling as needed.

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for fleet-related questions, issues, and day-to-day operational support.

 - Respond to and help escalate accidents, breakdowns, roadside issues, and other fleet incidents, including documentation and follow-up.

 - Work closely with the Senior Manager of Remote Operations and internal partners to ensure fleet operations align with production and event needs.



KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:

 - 3+ years of experience in fleet dispatch, transportation coordination, fleet compliance, logistics, or a related operations role.

 - Experience with vehicle maintenance scheduling, driver documentation, incident tracking, and operational recordkeeping.

 - Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

 - Ability to manage multiple priorities and adapt quickly in a fast-moving operational environment.

 - Excellent communication skills and the ability to work effectively with drivers, operations staff, vendors, and leadership.

 - Proficiency with spreadsheets, reporting tools, and digital tracking systems.

 - Availability to respond to weekend calls, after-hours issues, and on-call situations when required.



BONUS POINTS:

 - Experience in motorsports, racing, live event logistics, broadcast operations, or similar environments.

 - Familiarity with FloSports remote operations, event logistics, and trailer-based production support.

 - Experience creating or managing fleet scorecards, compliance dashboards, or weekly reporting.

 - Prior experience hiring, onboarding, or coordinating drivers.

source_jobs.title: Senior User Experience Researcher
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE:

This role will be part of a small, dedicated UX Research team.



You will get a lot of variety and see a lot of impact. We cover 25 sports and have products for web, mobile, and connected TV. This is an opportunity to investigate a wide range of users in multiple verticals using a varied set of methodologies and to do research that directly impacts users and products and results in real, tangible changes.



You will perform both generative and evaluative research. Studies will be both moderated and unmoderated, as appropriate. You will help determine what is appropriate. Generative studies may include interviews for persona and journey map development, longitudinal diary studies, card sorts, tree tests, etc. Evaluative studies may include baseline studies of existing products and iterative validation of new and improving products. Eventually, you may be conducting onsite contextual inquiries at locations ranging from dirt race tracks to cheer competitions. We will probably co-invent some hybrid solutions to get at tricky questions. All research source material and reports will live in a tagged repository, where an actively managed taxonomy of tags will help keep insights and artifacts persistently available and relevant. You will own the taxonomy.



On the quantitative side, you will help analyze behavioral analytics, design and interpret surveys, and interpret and present quantitative results from unmoderated usability studies.



You will be mentored and you will be expected to mentor more junior researchers.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Plan and conduct iterative usability tests

 - Plan and conduct UX research projects, including initial concept, method selection, execution, analysis, synthesis, reporting, and archiving.

 - Own and maintain the tagging taxonomy

 - Mentor junior researchers



KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:

 - Experience with quantitative and qualitative research methods

 - 5+ years of experience in UX research

 - Excellent presentation skills

 - Bachelor’s degree in relevant field

 - Relevant portfolio

 - Ability to drive definition and certainty in an ambiguous environment

 - Willingness to travel



BONUS POINTS:

 - Master’s degree in relevant field

 - Experience with a research repository system like EnjoyHQ, Dovetail, Condens, Aurelius, etc.

 - Experience with UX research platforms and tools like Maze, dscout, Optimal Workshop, Lookback, UserZoom, etc.

 - Experience with behavioral analytics platforms like Mixpanel, Pendo, Heap, Amplitude, etc.

 - Experience with Figma

 - Awareness of Agile and how to work in an Agile system

 - Experience with both B2C and B2B research studies



OUR COMMITMENT TO DIVERSITY:

FloSports exists to elevate the communities, athletes, and sports that have too often been overlooked. That mission starts with our own team. We believe that a diverse, inclusive workplace—one where different perspectives are sought out, heard, and valued—is essential to building a company that can truly serve the full spectrum of sports fans.



We are commit

source_jobs.title: Director of Performance Marketing
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE:

As a key member of the Marketing leadership team, the Director of Performance Marketing will lead FloSports’ Paid Media function, driving subscriber growth through highly analytical, performance-driven strategies. This Austin-based role reports to the VP of Growth and owns the end-to-end performance of paid channels, with a particular emphasis on Paid Search alongside a strong Paid Social program.



This is a hands-on leadership role within a complex and highly seasonal business. Success requires constant evaluation, optimization, and budget reallocation to capture demand across sports verticals and customer segments. You will not be stepping into a set-it-and-forget-it environment. Instead, you will play a critical role in shaping strategy in real time and ensuring the business is capitalizing on every growth opportunity throughout the year.



You will bring deep expertise in media mix modeling, budget management, and attribution, along with the ability to step back and see the broader growth picture while remaining close to execution. FloSports already has a strong ROI foundation, and this role is focused on unlocking the next phase of growth by scaling efficiently and identifying new opportunities.



The ideal candidate is highly analytical, exceptionally organized, and comfortable operating in a fast-paced environment with a high degree of complexity. You take ownership of outcomes, translate data into clear and actionable insights, and lead teams to test, learn, and scale what works. Most importantly, you are excited by the opportunity to make a meaningful impact and help unlock the full potential of FloSports.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Own and lead FloSports’ Paid Media strategy across Paid Search and Paid Social, managing and mentoring a small, high-impact team while remaining deeply hands-on in execution.

 - Drive subscriber acquisition through scalable, data-driven strategies with a strong focus on long-term value. Optimize toward LTV-based performance, ensuring efficient and sustainable growth across ROAS, CAC, and CPA.

 - Fully own and manage large-scale media budgets, ensuring effective allocation across channels, audiences, and sports verticals. Continuously evaluate performance to identify opportunities for greater efficiency, incremental revenue, and channel expansion.

 - Monitor performance daily and weekly, delivering clear and consistent reporting. Identify gaps, diagnose root causes, and develop actionable strategies to close those gaps and improve overall performance.

 - Play a critical role during key seasonal moments and major events, adjusting strategy in real time to capture demand. Provide additional oversight during high-priority periods, including occasional weekends and holidays as needed.

 - Partner closely with channel leads to optimize campaigns, test new tactics, and unlock incremental growth opportunities. Lead by example as a hands-on operator who can dive into the details when needed.

 - Define KPIs and success metrics aligned to both short-term acquisition goals

source_jobs.title: Summer Internship - Cybersecurity
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



ROLE:

The Cybersecurity Intern will work alongside FloSports' Application Security Engineer and Cybersecurity Analyst on day-to-day security work. The role is designed for someone early in their career who wants real exposure to a working cybersecurity program — identity, cloud security, endpoint protection, audits, and security tooling. Based in the Waterloo office, hybrid with a minimum of 3 days in-office. Reports to the IT Manager.



We'll match work to your skill level and stretch you as you grow.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Assist the cybersecurity team with day-to-day work across identity, cloud, and endpoint security.

 - Help investigate security findings and contribute to remediation plans.

 - Support ongoing audit and compliance efforts — evidence gathering, documentation, follow-ups.

 - Maintain documentation for security tools, processes, and policies.

 - Take ownership of small, well-scoped projects to build portfolio-worthy work.

 - Document what you learn so it benefits future team members.



KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:

 - Currently enrolled in an undergraduate program in computer science, cybersecurity, information systems, or related field.

 - Self-sufficient — can take a task, figure out what's needed, and ship it without daily hand-holding.

 - Foundational understanding of security concepts: CIA triad, common vulnerability classes, basic networking.

 - Comfort with at least one scripting language (Python, JavaScript, or bash).

 - Familiarity with cloud infrastructure concepts, ideally AWS.

 - Identity & access management basics.

 - Strong written communication — most of your impact will live in tickets, runbooks, and documentation.

 - Curiosity and willingness to push back. We don't want a quiet intern.

 - Available to work from our Waterloo office a minimum of 3 days per week.



BONUS POINTS:

 - Coursework or self-study in cloud security, AWS Cloud Practitioner, or Security+ in progress.

 - Familiarity with Confluence, Jira, or other documentation tools.

 - Experience with any IDP (Okta, Entra, Google).

 - CTF participation, home lab, or other "you built something because you wanted to" evidence.



 - Paid Internship - $22 CAD per hour

 - 20 - 30 hours per week

 - In office requirement at Waterloo, ON location

source_jobs.title: Broadcast Engineer, Level 1
source_jobs.description:
FloSports is the leading digital platform for underserved sports, combining live and on-demand streaming, original programming, data, and technology to help sports grow their audiences and build sustainable businesses, from youth to professional levels. By bringing thousands of events and communities onto one platform, Flo creates scale where none previously existed, connecting passionate fans with the sports they love while giving athletes, organizers, and rights holders the tools to reach and engage a global audience.



Flo streams more than 50,000 live events annually across sports, including motorsports, wrestling, hockey, track & field, cheer, jiu-jitsu, NCAA Division II and III athletics, and more. We’re looking for people who are passionate about sports, embody accountability, collaboration, curiosity, positivity, grit, and excellence, and are motivated to help grow and elevate the communities we serve.



THE ROLE:

We seek a skilled and passionate Broadcast Engineer Level I to join our team and support our streaming and broadcast engineering operations. This role will primarily focus on providing Tier 2 support for live events and will also involve key tasks such as testing with league partners, participating in R&D projects, and collaborating with Broadcast Operations, Logistics, Production, and Engineering teams. This role has a knack for streaming technology and enjoys working in a dynamic live environment.



 

RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Responsible for Tier 2 support on live events

 - Flexible with shift scheduling, work over weekends/nights

 - Assist with Feed Partners testing

 - Work with Logistics and Production for upcoming flo-produced events

 - Collaborate with Stream Operations on improving day to day processes

 - Triaging for escalation and documenting initial root causes in real-time

 - Possible travel for encoder installation & testing

 

KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & ABILITIES:

 - Basic understanding of streaming protocols (RTMP, SRT, RTP, WebRTC, Zixi)

 - Basic understanding of encoding softwares (vMix, OBS, Tricaster, etc)

 - Basic understanding of encoding hardware (TVU, Videon, Haivision, etc)

 - Basic networking knowledge

 - Basic understanding of analog and digital signal flows

 - Basic understanding on Zixi/ZEN Master

 - Basic understanding on AWS services (Medialive, Mediatailor, MediaStore, S3, EC2)

 - Collaborate with team members on Root Cause Analysis

 - Basic skills in alerting/monitoring tools such as NPAW YouBora, Orion, DataDog, ZEN Master

 - Basic skills in operational tools such as Jira, Confluence, Salesforce, Google Workspace, VoIP, Domo

 - Track/Resolve bugs on encoders/cellular bonding devices

 - Basic understanding of CDNs (Fastly, Cloudflare, Akamai, Cloudfront)

 

REQUIRED EXPERIENCE:

 - 1+ years in Remote Production

 - 3+ years in Live Event Support

 

BONUS POINTS:

 - A+ Certified

 - Network + Certified

 - AWS Solution Architect Associate (SAA)

 - 1 year of live production on-site (Produce, Direct, TD, GFX Op, EVS, etc)

 - Hands-on experience with TVU

 - Hands-on experience with Videon

 - 1+ year of stream technician on-site/remote



OUR COMMITMENT TO DIVERSITY:

FloSports exists to elevate the communities, athletes, and sports that have too often been overlooked. That mission starts with our own team. We believe that a diverse, inclusive workplace—one where different perspectives are sought out, heard, and valued—is essential to building a company that can truly serve the full spectrum of sports fans.



We are committed to creating a fair and equitable environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive. To help mitigate bias and expand opportunity, FloSports uses a blind recruiting process that supports more objective, inclusive hiring decisions. We’re intentional in our practices because we believe the best ideas and innovations come from teams that reflect the diversity of the world around us.



OUR BENEFITS:

 - Competitive employer paid benefits

notes: FloSports is a sports media company with a strong engineering focus, evident from the high number of tech roles posted.
